description: A graphql-middleware plugin for schema-agnostic CASL permission rules on GraphQL resolvers, declared per type/field in a PermissionsMap and enforced before each resolver runs.
rules: The library is schema-agnostic: subject names and condition types are derived from the consumer's generated Resolvers / ResolversTypes. Never hardcode domain type names in the library core.

All public exports come from src/index.ts: runtime helpers (createCan, createTyped, createSubjects, accept, deny, applyPermissions, Actions, abilityOptions) plus type helpers (PermissionsMap, Rule, SubjectName, SubjectMap, ArgsOf, etc.).

Runtime subject detection uses __typename via abilityOptions.detectSubjectType. Consumers tag plain objects with createTyped() rather than CASL's subject(). Always pass abilityOptions when building an ability with createMongoAbility.

createCan(getAbility, isAuthenticated, buildSubject?) returns a requireCan(action, subject, getSubjectData?) rule builder bound to the consumer's context and ability builder; getSubjectData reads condition values from resolver args.
accept is the always-pass primitive; deny is the always-fail primitive. A failed authentication check throws 'Not authenticated'; a failed ability check throws 'Forbidden'.
applyPermissions(schema, permissions) wraps graphql-middleware's applyMiddleware and keeps permissions typed as PermissionsMap<Resolvers> so mistyped type or field names are caught at compile time.
Stack: TypeScript 5 strict, ESM only, Vitest for tests, Biome for lint/format. No runtime dependencies — @casl/ability >=6, graphql >=16, and graphql-middleware >=6 are peer deps.
Use Conventional Commits; commits drive automated semantic-release publishing (feat = minor, fix = patch, BREAKING CHANGE = major).
